description: WhatsApp cold outreach templates by service angle — Aixer Solutions
rules: Under 100 words total — WhatsApp is conversational, not a pitch deck
One specific reference to their company or signal (not generic)
Close with one simple question — not a demand
Sign off: first name + company only (no email address in WhatsApp)
No corporate jargon: no 'leverage', 'synergy', 'transformation journey', 'I hope this message finds you well'
Short paragraphs with line breaks — easier to read on mobile
Do NOT include a subject line

variable_guide.{signal}: Specific recent signal (1 sentence, numbers if possible)
variable_guide.{pain_point}: The specific problem this signal implies
variable_guide.{outcome}: What Aixer Solutions would fix in plain language

process_automation.trigger: Operational headcount growing faster than revenue; manual bottleneck visible
process_automation.body:
Hi {first_name},

Saw that {company} {signal} — that level of growth usually means manual processes start becoming the constraint.

We work with Singapore {sector} businesses to automate those workflows, typically cutting ops overhead by 25–40% in 6–8 weeks.

Would it be useful to see what that could look like for {company}?

Hon Mun
Aixer Solutions

supply_chain_visibility.trigger: Logistics or inventory challenges; warehouse expansion; new distribution points
supply_chain_visibility.body:
Hi {first_name},

Noticed {company} {signal} — at that scale, inventory visibility usually becomes the first thing that breaks.

We build real-time supply chain AI for Singapore {sector} companies — no enterprise ERP price tag, deployed in weeks.

Open to a quick walkthrough of what this looks like?

Hon Mun
Aixer Solutions

customer_ai.trigger: High customer volume growth; support team hiring; B2C or e-commerce expansion
customer_ai.body:
Hi {first_name},

{company} {signal} — that kind of customer growth tends to bring support costs up in parallel.

We help Singapore {sector} businesses handle 60–80% of customer queries with AI agents, so the team focuses on the complex cases.

Want me to put together a 2-page overview of what this could look like for {company}?

Hon Mun
Aixer Solutions

multi_agent_orchestration.trigger: Cross-department coordination overhead; rapid scaling across multiple functions
multi_agent_orchestration.body:
Hi {first_name},

{company}'s {signal} tells me you're managing complexity across multiple teams — and that coordination overhead rarely shows up cleanly in any one budget.

We design AI systems for Singapore {sector} businesses that automate the handoffs between ops, finance, and customer teams.

Worth 15 minutes to see if this applies to {company}?

Hon Mun
Aixer Solutions

digital_transformation.trigger: Legacy systems; new leadership with tech mandate; digital transformation announcement
digital_transformation.body:
Hi {first_name},

{signal} at {company} is a strong signal that something has to change — but most transformation projects stall because they start with systems instead of outcomes.

We deploy AI agents for Singapore {sector} businesses that show real results in 6–8 weeks, not 18 months.

Happy to share what's worked for similar-sized businesses here in Singapore?

Hon Mun
Aixer Solutions

do: Peer-to-peer — you're a consultant, not a vendor
Specific company references feel relevant, not spammy
Short questions feel like a conversation starter
Plain English — no industry consultant jargon
do_not: Do not use emojis in first contact
Do not include pricing or package details
Do not list multiple service options
Do not use bullet points in WhatsApp messages
Do not cc or attach anything — WhatsApp is conversational
